title: Chinese steel flows face ongoing and new headwinds
section_path: Chinese steel flows face ongoing and new headwinds
section_path_text: Chinese steel flows face ongoing and new headwinds
summary:
Main
Steel flows slip into negative growth in April 2026
Steel Tonne-Miles
Image reference: unnamed.png | assets/2026-05-28_chinese-steel-flows-face-ongoing-and-new-headwinds_img_unnamed_0dc8f41d81e8.png
Key Takeaways
● Global steel flows soften 8% y/y in April 2026. ● Flows originating in China increased proportionally to 45%, from a long-run average of 38%, but in tonnage terms fell by 4% to 10mt. ● Flows from outside of China have decreased by 11%.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
Steel flows slip into negative growth in April 2026
Steel Tonne-Miles
Image reference: unnamed.png | assets/2026-05-28_chinese-steel-flows-face-ongoing-and-new-headwinds_img_unnamed_0dc8f41d81e8.png
Key Takeaways
● Global steel flows soften 8% y/y in April 2026. ● Flows originating in China increased proportionally to 45%, from a long-run average of 38%, but in tonnage terms fell by 4% to 10mt.

children.title: Near-term outlook remains challenging
children.summary: Looking ahead, the near-term outlook for seaborne steel flows remains challenging, with headwinds compounding on multiple fronts. The Strait of Hormuz disruption shows little sign of near-term resolution, and with Chinese exports to the Arabian Gulf running at a fraction of typical volumes, a swift recovery in overall flow tonnage looks unlikely by the end of Q2.

children.title: China's steel exports face mounting challenges
section_path: China's steel exports face mounting challenges
children.section_path_text: China's steel exports face mounting challenges
children.summary: April 2026 marks a notable inflection point for seaborne steel markets. A confluence of geopolitical disruption and domestic cost pressures has pushed global flows into negative growth territory, with little near-term relief in sight.
